notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A break and hold below the $254 52w low, or RSI failing to hold 30, would confirm downtrend continuation and flip this to a neutral/trim.

positional.falsifier: Next earnings B/B printing <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or op margin failing to recover above ~12%, would confirm structural erosion and flip this to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E confirmed above 23x AND op margin failing to recover toward 12%+ over the next two prints would validate a real premium and push this to a stronger trim; conversely margin recovery + backlog expansion flips it negative (buy).

tactical.falsifier: RSI>75 plus two down days on volume.

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E<25x flips to buy; broad capex cuts + B2B collapse flip to strong trim.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 35 on accelerating volume with LIN decisively losing its SMA50 ($511.94) would flip this to a clear buy-the-dip tactical signal; conversely a spike above 70 into a blow-off would flip it to trim.

positional.falsifier: LIN closing below its SMA200 ($470.69) or the BBB OAS gapping wider by >25bps (project-IRR headwind) would flip this positive/trim; a fresh 52w high on expanding backlog would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A hydrogen mega-project win >$1B with a named creditworthy take-or-pay offtaker (steel/ammonia/refining), OR the peer premium compressing further toward 0% on unchanged fundamentals, would flip this to a clear buy; conversely a genuine like-for-like price/mix ex-FX turning negative would confirm real moat erosion and deepen the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close back above SMA50 ($404.40) on expanding volume with RSI reclaiming 55 would flip the tactical bias from oversold-buy to broken-momentum-neutral/sell (or vice versa, a break below the $349.20 52w low would deepen conviction).

positional.falsifier: A reclaim of SMA200 ($442.90) confirming trend repair would validate max-conviction buy; conversely, a break below the $349.20 52w low accompanied by Azure CC deceleration below 22% would confirm the value trap and flip positional to neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth decelerating below 22% on the next earnings print, or a materially adverse OpenAI partnership renegotiation, would flip the strategic thesis from discounted-compounder-buy to expectations-reset-neutral/trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp 10Y move (>15bps in a day) that drives ENB down 2%+ on no fundamental news would flip this to a tactical buy on rate overreaction.

positional.falsifier: The 10Y falling ~40bps (toward 4.1%) or ENB dropping ~5% to push the yield spread back above 150bps would flip this to a positional buy.

strategic.falsifier: A structural re-rating catalyst — either a start to a rate-cut cycle re-widening the yield spread above 300bps, or a major accretive LNG/gas volume expansion — would flip this to a strategic buy.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day SMA ($12.6) on rising volume would flip this from a dip-buy to a broken-trend sell (positive score).

positional.falsifier: COPX-GLNCY spread flipping to GLNCY-leading (positive spread) while copper stalls would remove the catch-up edge and neutralize/flip the score.

strategic.falsifier: Chinese PMI/property data deteriorating sharply and LME copper breaking down through support (dragging COPX lower) would signal the trough is deepening, flipping the strategic score positive.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking decisively below 35 with expanding down-volume (flips me to stronger buy) or above 60 reclaiming the 50DMA on strong ETF inflows (flips me to neutral/trim).

positional.falsifier: Sustained multi-day IBIT net outflows accelerating alongside a break below $32.84 on rising volume would confirm broken structure and flip this to neutral/trim; conversely already firmly on buy side.

strategic.falsifier: Concrete evidence the ETF-driven demand regime is reversing (sustained cumulative net outflows across issuers, hostile regulatory reversal) would flip this to a trim; a decisive new-adoption catalyst would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp MXN move (>2% in a session) or a Banxico rate surprise that resets the short-term regime would flip this off neutral.

positional.falsifier: A negative volume/margin surprise at next earnings or a MXN break above ~18.5 would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: A durable MXN depreciation regime (toward 20+) driven by capital flight or US-Mexico trade/tariff conflict, or an adverse Mexican tax/regulatory hit to margins, would flip this to a trim.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp RSI move above 60 with a decisive break back above the SMA50 ($18.69) on volume would flip this to a sell (overbought) or confirm momentum reversal.

positional.falsifier: A confirmed board decision to materially cut ordinary dividends or a hostile capex mandate would turn the discount into a justified derating, flipping the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained WTI move toward/above $85 combined with an official reaffirmation of the shareholder-return/dividend framework would flip strategic to a buy; conversely a formal dividend cut confirms the trim.

tactical.falsifier: WCI composite reversing sharply higher (>10% week-over-week) on a fresh Suez closure would flip this to a buy; a break back below the 50-day on falling rates flips it to a clear trim.

positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0.5 alongside a WCI decline would flip positional negative (trim); a sustained GSCPI move above 1.75 with rising rates would push it firmly buy-side.

strategic.falsifier: P/E compressing toward 5-8x (earnings spiking to peak) or P/B rising well above 1.0 would flip this to a trim; conversely a Logistics spin-off announcement or P/B falling below 0.55 would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A close back below the SMA50 ($476) or RSI dropping under 40 on a biotech (XBI) reversal would flip this to a sell/avoid tactical stance.

positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling back below +10% (funding thaw stalling) plus TMO losing the SMA50 would flip positional to neutral/sell.

strategic.falsifier: Forward guidance revised down / organic growth failing to re-accelerate off the ~1% trough (staying flat or negative into 2026), or NIH/China capex cuts confirmed, would flip strategic to neutral/sell.

tactical.falsifier: A close back below the 50-day SMA ($101.46) on expanding volume with cohort relative strength turning negative would flip this to a sell/neutral tactical.

positional.falsifier: A quarterly print showing cRPO decelerating below 18% with soft Now Assist monetization, or a sustained down-trend of EPS revisions, would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.falsifier: Real rate falling sustainably below 0.5% (Fed pivot) alongside continued cRPO strength would flip this negative (buy).

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close above the 200d SMA ($530) on expanding volume would flip this to a clearer buy; conversely a break back below the 50d ($499) toward the 52w low would deepen the drawdown and strengthen the tactical buy — either way the sign moves as the drawdown resolves.

positional.falsifier: A cross-border volume print below ~6% or negative EPS revisions over the next 90 days would flip this positive (trim); conversely reclaiming the 200d with a double-digit cross-border confirmation would deepen the buy.

strategic.falsifier: Interchange/network-fee legislation moving to 'passed' or a named major merchant adopting stablecoin settlement at checkout (material evidence) would flip this to a trim; conversely the twin premium re-widening toward 15-20% on continued VAS acceleration confirms the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A break and sustained close below the $396.68 52w low on rising volume, or a pre-print negative pre-announcement, would flip the tactical to trim/positive.

positional.falsifier: A downward EPS revision cluster (30d/90d turning negative) or management quantifying the 2027 transition as material would flip this positive; conversely a reclaim of the 50-day ($425) confirms the buy.

strategic.falsifier: Management quantifying the 2027 transition as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue), OR measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo/Ottava, would flip this to trim.
